evidence: The paper applies Boruta (RF-based importance) for feature selection: "included when the Boruta [28] algorithm was applied" alongside DESeq2 testing. However, the main conclusions are: "median required sample sizes were 480 (XGB)/190 (RF)/269 (NN)" and "Higher effect sizes, less class imbalance/dispersion, and less complex data were associated with lower required sample size." These conclusions rest on measured AUC from learning curves and regression coefficients of independently-measured dataset characteristics, not on importance magnitude or ranking. Boruta provides a robust yes/no feature selection call (not magnitude interpretation), corroborated by parallel DESeq2 and external validation on held-out datasets.
